This week, I completed development for story TA-869. During development, I encountered an error while adding the driver due to Tosca being unable to scan a specific element. I identified the root cause and successfully resolved the issue. After completing the development, I moved on to testing for TA-869. In parallel, I also began the development work for TA-870.
Learned this week: This week, I gained practical experience in troubleshooting element scanning issues in Tosca and successfully resolving them during script development. The process helped deepen my understanding of how Tosca interacts with various application elements. Additionally, managing the transition between development, defect resolution, and testing further strengthened my ability to handle automation tasks efficiently.
Future work plans: Next week, I plan to complete the testing phase for TA-869 and make any necessary adjustments based on the results. I will continue with the development of TA-870 and ensure it is ready for testing as well. My focus will be on maintaining the quality of automation scripts and ensuring both stories are ready for further regression or integration testing as needed.

Work this week: Focused on the creation and customization of Quote Cover Letters for the Admitted Auto and Admitted Package programs. This included aligning the cover letters with the specific requirements for each program, ensuring accurate display of coverage details, limits, and other critical data. Collaborated with internal teams to gather necessary inputs and incorporated dynamic tags to reflect coverage selections. Addressed formatting and alignment issues to enhance the professional presentation of the documents, ensuring they meet both client expectations and internal compliance standards.
Learned this week:
Gained deeper insight into the structure and key components of Quote Cover Letters, particularly for the Admitted Auto and Admitted Package programs. This included understanding how to effectively communicate program-specific details, ensure compliance with regulatory standards, and tailor content to meet client and market expectations. Additionally, I enhanced my collaboration and document refinement skills through iterative feedback cycles with internal teams.
Also, Learned about the Home state Rule that is applied to the E&S programs. This tells us how the Taxes will behave for the E&S Programs for Multi-state Policies.
Future work plans:
Next week, the focus will shift to developing the Quote Cover Letters for the E&S Package and Admitted Package programs. This will involve gathering program-specific requirements, identifying necessary dynamic tags, and ensuring the cover letters accurately reflect the coverage options, limits, and deductibles selected in the system. We will also ensure consistency in formatting and compliance with underwriting guidelines, while collaborating with relevant stakeholders to validate content accuracy and usability.
Also, I will be working on setting up the Taxes for the E&S Programs based on the Home State Rule for all the states.

Work this week:
Worked on Re-Testing of the JIRA tickets raised during the Parallel Testing - Re-tested the fixed tickets in the UAT Environment and ensured that the current fixes do not hamper the existing functionality of the system. After restesting updated the Tickets Status in JIRA as per the Re-testing results. Once Ticket is deployed to PROD perform the Smoke Test  on PROD which involves verifying the stability of key functionalities within the allowed constraints. Since we cannot create new records or modify existing data in the production environment,  validated the deployed changes by performing a limited-scope sanity check. This includes checking the impacted modules or screens , primarily covering UI, navigation, access permissions, and any specific areas touched by the latest release.
I attended Weekly Project Updates Meetings to Share the update and to stay aligned with the team's progress
Learned this week:
Through this task, I developed a deeper understanding of the real-time QA lifecycle and the importance of maintaining system stability post-deployment. Key learnings include -
Gained hands-on experience in UAT Re-testing, ensuring that fixes do not impact existing features.Improved proficiency in using JIRA for tracking ticket status and collaborating across teams.Understood the criticality of Smoke Testing in PROD, where validation needs to be precise and low-risk.Learned to identify and validate changes through limited-scope sanity checks, focusing on UI, navigation, permissions, and impacted modules.Enhanced my communication and collaboration skills by actively participating in weekly project update meetings, ensuring alignment with the broader team goals.
Future work plans:
Take initiative in test case creation and optimization, especially for regression and sanity testing scenarios.
Collaborate more with the dev and product teams to identify bugs early and improve release quality.
Continue upskilling to make better defect reports
